title: CHILD CARE REIMBURSEMENT FOR RESOURCE PARENTS AND RELATIVE CAREGIVERS - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE CHILD CARE REIMBURSEMENT FOR RESOURCE PARENTS AND RELATIVE CAREGIVERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Child Care Reimbursement for resource parents and relative caregivers is intended to help pay child care costs. Certified resource parents and relative caregivers who, due to employment or attending college or other educational programs, require child care for children placed in their homes qualify for reimbursement. Payments are issued as reimbursement to the resource parent and can be up to $375 a month per child. The reimbursement may only be used when the children are served by licensed or approved childcare settings. If the provider is a relative or family friend who is not required to be licensed, DHS must approve that provider. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Resource parents and relative caregivers who are parenting children in DHS custody 12 years of age and younger -Children older than 12 who have an identified reason for the need for supervision may qualify for remimbursement -Reimbursment only covers child care costs incurred due to employment or if the provider is attending college or other educational programs. If the certified family is two certified adults, both adults must be employed or attending an educational program. Hours: Varies by DHS branch Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers INTAKE PROCEDURE: Resource parents and relative caregivers should contact the child's caseworker about their child care needs. Once an approved child care provider is identified, the family can fill out the Child Care Authorization form. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Contact caseworker for documentation requirements AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Child Care Expense Assistance * Foster Children Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - CHILDREN, YOUTH AND FAMILIES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FOSTER OR ADOPTIVE PARENT INQUIRY LINE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORT A FOSTER CHILD If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE
